title: olea gitingensis
intro: Olea gitingensis, also known as the Philippine olive tree or Philippine wild olive, is a species of evergreen tree native to the Philippines. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating wounds, fever, and rheumatism, due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Olea gitingensis include reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and promoting wound healing. In herbal preparations, the leaves and branches of Olea gitingensis are often used to make teas, infusions, and topical applications, and the oil extracted from the fruit is used in aromatherapy.
